description: Train a MultitaskRegressor to predict aqueous solubility from SMILES (Delaney/ESOL ECFP benchmark by default, or a custom CSV), evaluate on train/valid/test, and optionally predict new molecules.
rules: read-only / local — reads a CSV (or downloads the Delaney benchmark) and writes a report; no remote or live service is mutated
scaffold splitting is used for custom CSVs to avoid molecular data leakage
report (training log, evaluation metrics, predictions) written to record_store
needs deepchem + a DL backend (torch/tensorflow) + RDKit at run time; porter degrades gracefully if absent
usage: Leave DATA_CSV empty to use the Delaney (ESOL) benchmark, or set it to a SMILES CSV with SMILES_COL + TARGET_COL. Set PREDICT_SMILES (space-separated) to predict after training. Output: solubility.txt.
limitation: Not hermetic offline — full training requires deepchem, a deep-learning backend, and RDKit (and network access for the Delaney benchmark). Without them the porter writes solubility.txt noting the missing dependency and exits 0.
